question: What is the Section 80D tax deduction limit for a self/family health insurance premium if I am under 60?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse or say it cannot advise on Income Tax Act provisions — the policy corpus does not include Section 80D limits.

question: If I pay the premium for my parents who are 65 and 68, how much can I claim as a deduction under Section 80D?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse or say it cannot advise on Income Tax Act deductions — 80D senior-citizen limits are not stated in any policy document in the corpus.

question: Can I claim a deduction under Section 80DDB for cancer treatment expenses?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse — Section 80DDB rules are part of the Income Tax Act and are not covered in the policy corpus.

question: I have a 40% disability certificate — what is my Section 80U deduction?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse — Section 80U personal-disability deductions are an Income Tax Act provision, not a health-policy detail; the corpus does not contain ITA limits.

question: What is the current GST rate charged on health insurance premiums in India?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse or say the policy documents do not state the applicable GST percentage — GST rates are set by the GST Council, not the insurer's product document.

question: If I buy a top-up health plan in addition to my base policy, is the top-up premium also eligible for tax deduction?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse on tax-eligibility advice — deduction eligibility under Section 80D is governed by the Income Tax Act, not the policy document; the bot can only describe policy features.

question: Are maturity proceeds from my health insurance policy tax-free under Section 10(10D)?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse or clarify that Section 10(10D) applies to life-insurance proceeds and is outside the health-policy corpus; health indemnity policies do not pay maturity proceeds.

question: The plan is called Tax Gain — what specific Section 80D tax deduction amount can I claim by buying this policy?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse or note that the Tax Gain policy document in the corpus does not state any Section 80D limit — deduction eligibility and amounts are governed by the Income Tax Act, not the policy document.

question: If I pay ₹15,000 premium for Tax Gain at a 30% tax bracket, exactly how much tax will I save in the financial year?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse — the Tax Gain policy document does not contain Section 80D limits or marginal tax-rate guidance; tax-saving calculations require Income Tax Act knowledge that is outside the corpus.

question: Will TDS be deducted on the cashless claim amount paid by the insurer to the hospital?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse or say TDS treatment of insurance claim payouts is governed by the Income Tax Act and is not addressed in the policy document corpus.

question: If I pay a 2-year premium in lump sum in March, can I claim the entire amount as a Section 80D deduction in the same financial year?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse — single-premium vs. multi-year apportionment rules under Section 80D are Income Tax Act provisions; the policy corpus does not include tax-treatment of lump-sum premiums.

question: Can I claim HRA exemption and Section 80D health insurance deduction together in the same year?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse — HRA exemption rules and inter-section deduction interactions are Income Tax Act provisions outside the policy corpus.

question: How much of my preventive health check-up cost can I claim as a deduction under Section 80D, separately from the premium?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse on the deduction-amount question — the policy corpus describes preventive check-up coverage limits, but Section 80D sub-limit for preventive HCU (₹5,000 within the overall cap) is an Income Tax Act provision not stated in the documents.

question: If I pay my health insurance premium in cash, can I still claim the Section 80D deduction?
expected_answer: Bot should refuse — the rule that 80D deductions require non-cash payment (except for preventive check-up) is an Income Tax Act provision; the policy documents in the corpus do not state payment-mode rules for tax eligibility.
